scope: Complete retail Fallout: New Vegas data in flat OpenMW, beginning with a normal level-one Goodsprings session and ending with the retail intro and Doc Mitchell character creation.
greenRule: Every subsystem must be pass on the same promoted OpenMW queue with natural authored state, current FNV/FO3/Morrowind controls, native background captures, exact provenance, and no staged references, substitute player, actor neutralization, dry cell, forced proof weather/image-space, diagnostic chatter, or unresolved visual review.

normalSessionBaseline.failure: Session integrity now passes without implicit player equipment, but native visual review, authored inventory/equipment behavior and the retail HUD/Pip-Boy remain failing or unproven.
visualReview: fail: the authored vertex-alpha shadow plane in NV_ProspectorSaloon-Neon.NIF renders opaque black instead of blending over the Prospector Saloon facade
fail: the native Player body/equipment presentation is incomplete and not a retail level-one Courier
fail: the displayed HUD is the shared OpenMW fallback rather than the retail FNV HUD/Pip-Boy presentation
fail: sky, clouds, lighting and final color remain visibly non-retail and lack a same-frame oracle match
unproven: duplicate/invisible actor and object absence has not been exhaustively enumerated

subsystems.reason: Patch 0023 keeps the native retail Player proxy but applies no outfit or headgear without an explicit override or proof bootstrap; the isolated normal run reports zero override lines.
Level-one movement and camera machine gates pass, but scale and retail timing are not yet same-slice differential proven.
Radius-one cells load in the smoke run; traversal across boundaries and hitch timing are not yet measured against retail.

Patch 0021 proves the authored Goodsprings door destination completes preload before activation and proves interior return under isolated writable state with no cache-capacity warning, with FO3 and Morrowind controls; same-frame retail transition latency and hitch parity remain unmeasured.
The authored Easy Pete reference resolves naturally without neutralization; exhaustive duplicate/invisible identity and schedule coverage is incomplete.
Easy Pete skin/beard/hair/sideburn parity and the normal Player equipment/body presentation fail.
Bounded Easy Pete furniture evidence exists, but the natural-session timing and arbitrary activation path are not proven.
The natural session removes forced proof IDs and exposes visibly non-retail sky, lighting, final color and a black facade material.
No same-slice retail/OpenMW sound event, asset, mix or timing ledger exists.
A bounded driven audit exists; natural quest progression and exhaustive retail dialogue/result coverage do not.
The implicit proof outfit is removed, but authored starting inventory/equipment and inventory interactions are not acceptance-proven, and the native capture still uses fallback HUD presentation.
Benny shooting, Doc Mitchell wake-up, SPECIAL, skills, traits, appearance, controls and completion are not implemented as an acceptance path.
The oracle runner is isolated, but exhaustive per-record, per-draw, pixel and timing coverage is not yet present.
The isolated FO3 interaction control passes all actor, dialogue, door, interior, radio and return gates on the patch-0023 binary with no preloader-capacity warning.
The isolated Morrowind normal-session control passes movement, camera, authored Fargoth and three native captures on the patch-0023 binary.
Patches 0001-0023 replay from 9acf88c34b to exact checkpoint tree 82b7b3083932ccef1b6b401187bd56de1d1c06ed.
